expectations.text: Output contains exactly 3 use case sections
expectations.evidence: Three use cases present: Customer Support Agent, Inventory Sync, Returns Processing

expectations.text: Each use case includes at least one specific metric â€” a number, percentage, time range, or dollar range â€” in the ROI field
expectations.evidence: UC1: '$300+/week', '3+ hours/day'; UC2: '30-60 minutes/day', '$3,900/year'; UC3: '$750/month', '20-30% deflection rate'

expectations.text: Each use case contains a 'Hook:' line with an outreach-ready sentence in quotes
expectations.evidence: No 'Hook:' lines present. Uses 'ROI framing:' bullet sections instead. No outreach-ready one-liners in quotes.

expectations.text: Use cases cover at least two different dimensions (operational efficiency, revenue/growth, customer experience) â€” not all three on the same theme
expectations.evidence: Covers support (ops), inventory (revenue protection), returns (CX + ops) â€” 2+ dimensions covered even without explicit labeling

expectations.text: Use cases reference specific details from the input (e.g., Shopify, inventory, returns, support emails) â€” not generic 'AI automation' descriptions
expectations.evidence: References Shopify, spreadsheet, handmade jewelry production constraints, returns â€” all input-specific

expectations.text: Output ends with a quick pitch summary section containing 3 bullets
expectations.evidence: Ends with a Summary Table (not 3 bullets). No 'Quick pitch summary' section.
